scope: Function-level nodes and edges for the env->PutEnv $THISUSER bypass, the OnInclude callback path, and tlsFile::GetPathIfExists path-traversal handling.

nodes_added.name: tlsEnv::OnDoFunc PutEnv
nodes_added.summary: Script-callable env->PutEnv. No allowlist on the var name. Setting TMS_USER trivially forges $THISUSER.

nodes_added.name: tlsSocP::OnInclude
nodes_added.summary: Parse-time #include resolver. Splits Object::Event, looks up via tlsEnv::FindObject + tlsObject::EventGet. NOT a filesystem path; not directly attacker-controllable.

nodes_added.name: tlsFile::GetPathIfExists
nodes_added.summary: Path resolver. Three branches: TMS_MSK (cache-bounded), TMS_SCR (cache-bounded via GetScrFileInfo), all other env-vars (string-concat with no `..` normalisation).

nodes_added.name: tlsEnv::GetScrFileInfo
nodes_added.summary: TMS_SCR resolver. Walks TMS_SCR path list in order (Custom\scr first by default → customer overlay precedence). Matches built candidate path against m_lfileinfoScr cache (implicit allowlist of indexed files).

nodes_added.name: tlsEnv::GetMskFileInfo
nodes_added.summary: TMS_MSK resolver. Same cache-bounded pattern as GetScrFileInfo (not deep-read in this trace).

nodes_added.name: tlsEnv::LoadScrFileInfo
nodes_added.summary: Builds m_lfileinfoScr at startup by scanning TMS_SCR paths. Symlink/junction-point traversal behaviour not deep-read; flagged as open question.

edges_added.note: No allowlist; any var name accepted, including TMS_USER.
Setting TMS_USER changes what $THISUSER subsequently evaluates to — bypasses script-level authz checks gated on $THISUSER (existing review §M10).
Resolves Object portion of Object::Event. Not a filesystem operation.
Resolves Event portion. Returns the named event's program text. Not file IO.
Non-TMS_SCR/TMS_MSK branch builds path by string concat (lines 5736-5786) and calls Exists with no traversal check. Win32 resolves `..` natively.
Implicit allowlist of indexed files. Built once via LoadScrFileInfo.
Builds the cache at startup. Recursion / symlink semantics not deep-read.

notes: T05 resolves two of the existing review's open questions definitively: env->PutEnv is script-callable with no allowlist (M10 confirmed exploitable from any script context), and OnInclude is parse-time + object-event-keyed (not a filesystem traversal vector). It surfaces a sharper version of the M7/M11 path-traversal concern: non-TMS_SCR/TMS_MSK env-vars in GetPathIfExists have no normalisation and write-anywhere is possible if any wire-derived value reaches the sFileName argument.
